behavior_summary: U8P_DUMMY::look is a U8P_DUMMY::look/examine handler that, when the player examines the entity, plays a single player-facing bark text 'dummy ' and then suspends the process.
No branching, menus, global flags, or external calls are present; the routine is an informational response only.
The presence of a suspend indicates the U8P_DUMMY::look process is paused after delivering the bark (likely until resumed or the actor leaves).
notable_control_flow: Entry: invoked by a U8P_DUMMY::look/examine event (processType 0x0208).
Termination: suspend the process immediately after the bark.
open_questions: Is the process resumed later by another script or by the engine, or is suspend effectively an end-of-interaction pause for U8P_DUMMY::look handlers?
Is this handler tied to a fast-area trigger region (enterFastArea/leaveFastArea) in the game world or only to a direct 'U8P_DUMMY::look' action?
Are the raw Unknown nodes ('{' and 'suspend' remnants) encoding any additional micro-behavior that was lost in decompilation?

main_function.name: U8P_DUMMY::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examine/U8P_DUMMY::look response for the entity by showing a brief bark ('dummy ') and pausing the U8P_DUMMY::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by a player U8P_DUMMY::look/examine event (targeting or examining the NPC/object).
Inferred: associated with the engine's U8P_DUMMY::look-type process entry (processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_DUMMY::look
function_map.role: Responds to examine/U8P_DUMMY::look event by emitting a single bark and suspending the process.
